rule.title: Set Default umask for Users
rule.description: The umask controls the default permissions of newly created files. To ensure that files are created with restrictive default permissions, the umask should be set to 0077 or more restrictive.

test_results.evidence: umask setting is not restrictive enough

rule.title: Ensure SELinux Policy is Configured
rule.description: SELinux provides mandatory access control policies that protect the system from unauthorized access.

rule.title: Disable SSH Password Authentication
rule.description: SSH password authentication should be disabled in favor of key-based authentication to prevent brute force attacks.

test_results.evidence: PasswordAuthentication is enabled in SSH configuration

rule.title: Ensure Appropriate File Permissions
rule.description: File permissions should be restrictive to prevent unauthorized access.

rule.title: Windows Feature Configuration
rule.description: This rule is only applicable to Windows systems.
